mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, invalidating the stable inflation condition.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio for 20-day forward returns over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity that triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when industrial production falls by more than 0.5 percent month-over-month.
